prompt: Technical cutaway illustration on a dark background (#0a0a0a) showing a cross-section of moist soil below a grass surface. On the left, a magnesium block labelled 'sacrificial anode (−)' with arrows Mg → Mg²⁺ + 2e⁻ dissolving; on the right, a horizontal steel pipeline labelled 'pipeline (cathode, +)' with O₂ + 4H⁺ + 4e⁻ → 2H₂O at its surface. An insulated wire connects them above ground, with an electron-flow arrow from Mg to the pipe. Label: sacrificial anode (Mg), pipeline (Fe), e⁻, moist soil. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Underground diagram: a magnesium block buried in moist soil wired to a steel pipeline; electrons flow from Mg to the pipe; Mg dissolves as Mg2+ while the pipe is protected as the cathode

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). Left: a glowing chemical reaction vessel; an arrow of light flows rightward labelled faintly 'electrical energy' into a small spinning motor doing work. Above the flow, a subtle budget-gauge motif suggests a fixed maximum (free energy) that caps how much work can be drawn. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, amber-to-cyan energy flow on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text.
alt: A glowing flow from a chemical reaction into electrical energy into a turning motor, with a budget gauge labelling the maximum work

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). A horizontal chain of glowing nodes labelled with descending oxidation states of a metal, connected left-to-right by luminous arrows; each arrow carries a small voltage value floating above it. The leftmost node is the highest oxidation state, the rightmost is the neutral metal. The mood is 'a map of an element's redox landscape on one line'. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, cyan nodes and amber arrows on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No readable text beyond faint voltage hints.
alt: A horizontal chain of an element's oxidation states linked by glowing arrows, each arrow labelled with a reduction potential

prompt: Latimer diagram on a dark background (#0a0a0a). Three nodes in a horizontal row: 'Cu²⁺' (left), 'Cu⁺' (middle), 'Cu' (right). An arrow from Cu²⁺ to Cu⁺ labelled '+0.15 V' above and '1e⁻' below; an arrow from Cu⁺ to Cu labelled 'E = ?' above and '1e⁻' below. A long curved arrow spanning underneath from Cu²⁺ all the way to Cu, labelled '+0.34 V' and '2e⁻'. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Latimer diagram: Cu2+ connected by an arrow labelled 0.15 V (1 electron) to Cu+, then by an arrow labelled E (1 electron) to Cu; a long arrow below spans Cu2+ to Cu labelled 0.34 V (2 electrons)

prompt: Latimer-fragment diagram on a dark background (#0a0a0a). Three nodes: 'M²⁺', 'M⁺' (boxed/highlighted in the middle), 'M'. Arrow M²⁺ → M⁺ labelled '1.5 V'; arrow M⁺ → M labelled '2 V'. From the highlighted M⁺, a curved arrow looping left labelled 'oxidation' and a curved arrow looping right labelled 'reduction', illustrating self-oxidation and self-reduction (disproportionation). A small note 'E_cell = 2 − 1.5 = +0.5 V > 0'. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Latimer fragment: M2+ to M+ labelled 1.5 V, then M+ to M labelled 2 V; the middle species M+ is highlighted as disproportionating with oxidation arrow to the left and reduction arrow to the right

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). Left: a beaker densely packed with glowing ions; right: the same beaker sparse and dilute; between them a voltmeter whose needle visibly sits at different positions for each, connected by light. The mood is 'voltage tracks concentration'. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, cyan ions and amber needle glow on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No text.
alt: A voltmeter needle responding as the ion density in a beaker changes from dense to sparse, showing voltage tracking concentration

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). Three connected motifs across the width, all emanating from a single faint glowing equation in the centre-background: (left) a balanced scale symbolising equilibrium; (middle) two beakers of the same blue solution, one deep blue and one pale, wired together; (right) a sleek pH-meter probe dipping into liquid with a digital readout glow. The mood is 'one equation, three powers'. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, cyan and amber accents on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No readable text.
alt: Three glowing motifs — an equilibrium balance, two beakers of the same blue solution at different shades, and a pH meter probe — all springing from one equation

prompt: Ultra-wide cinematic banner (16:5 ratio). Left: a burette drips titrant into a beaker holding two electrodes wired to a glowing voltmeter. Right: a graph of voltage versus volume, flat then leaping sharply upward at a marked equivalence point, the jump glowing. The mood is 'the meter, not a dye, tells you when you're done'. Sleek scientific-cinematic style, amber curve and cyan electrode glow on a near-black background (#0a0a0a). No readable text.
alt: A burette dripping into a beaker with two electrodes and a voltmeter, beside a graph whose curve leaps sharply at the equivalence point

prompt: Potentiometric titration apparatus diagram on a dark background (#0a0a0a). A beaker of solution holds two electrodes side by side: one labelled 'indicator electrode', one labelled 'reference electrode', both wired up to a voltmeter (circle with V) at the top. A burette above the beaker is labelled 'titrant' with a drop falling. Label: indicator electrode, reference electrode, voltmeter, burette/titrant. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Apparatus: beaker of solution with an indicator electrode and a reference electrode, both connected to a voltmeter; a burette delivers titrant into the beaker

prompt: Two side-by-side graphs on a dark background (#0a0a0a). Graph (a): y-axis 'E', x-axis 'volume of titrant'; an S-shaped curve that is gently sloped then leaps steeply upward at a marked 'equivalence point' (dashed vertical line). Graph (b): y-axis 'ΔE/ΔV', x-axis 'volume of titrant'; a curve that rises to a sharp single peak aligned with the same equivalence-point dashed line, then falls. Orange accent labels and leader lines, clean technical illustration style. No bright background.
alt: Two graphs side by side: left, E versus volume showing a sigmoid with a steep step at the equivalence point; right, ΔE/ΔV versus volume showing a sharp peak at the same volume
